content.full_text:
Commissioll 
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322-5662 
John J. Carniato, Counsel 
Administration 
322-5660 
April 5, 1984 
Central Contra Costa Sanitary District 
3708 Mt. Diablo Boulevard 
Lafayette, CA 
94549 
Executive/legal 
322-5901 
Enforcement 
322-6<441 
Re 
Your Request for Advice 
Our No. A-84-053 
Dear Mr. Carniato: 
Your letter requests information regarding consultants being 
covered by an agency's conflict of interest code. 
You made 
specific reference to some prior correspondence with us on this 
subject. 
I enclose a copy of our letter to you and a later 
advice letter to Mr. Botz, whom you refer to in your letter. 
I 
hope these are helpful. 
I am also enclosing a copy of our instruction pamphlet 
regarding designating employees for a conflict of interest code, 
including consultants. 
In addition, I enclose a copy of an 
advice letter to David Kaplan (No. A-82-108) which discusses 
when a consultant is participating in making decisions. 
I hope 
that these documents will also assist you. 
If you have further 
questions, please do not hesitate to write with the specifics, 
we shall be happy to respond. 
REL:plh 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
~ 
-~---J. 
f~)!~~ 
~~:PYi~--) 
<:;.? 
./ 
/ Robert E. Leidi~ 
Counsel 
Legal Division 


LAW OFFICES 
JOHN J. CARNIATO 
JOHN J. eARN 
JAMES E. ALLEN 
G 
N 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Post Office Box 807 
Sacrmanento, California 95804 
Gentlemen: 
OAK HILL OFFICE PARK 
3708 MT. DiABLO BOULEVARD 
SiTE 300 
LAFAYETTE, CALIFORNIA 94549 
TELEPHONE: 14151 2e3~iS52 
March 7, 1984 
Sometime ago, on behalf of my client, Central Contra Costa 
Sanitary District, I requested information as to your opinion 
of the requirements of a public agency to require consultants 
of a public agency to file disclosure statements. 
You very kindly responded with some information and the pur-
pose of this letter is twofold: 
1. 
In your earlier letter you indicated that you had 
adopted a draft opinion "In the Matter of James Botz",County 
Counsel, County of Sonoma. 
At that time I did not receive 
that opinion and could you please send one now. 
2. 
Have any opinions been issued with regard to that 
issue? 
If the response is in the affirmative, would you 
please advise as to how I can gain access to those opinions. 
Thank you again for your courtesy and cooperation in this 
matter and others. 
JJC/pv

sections.question:
s, please do not hesitate to write with the specifics, 
we shall be happy to respond. 
REL:plh 
Enclosures
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks about whether consultants of a public agency must be covered by the agency's conflict of interest code and required to file disclosure statements, and requests copies of prior FPPC opinions on this topic.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC does not provide a direct substantive conclusion in this response letter, instead referring the requester to prior correspondence, an instruction pamphlet on designating employees for conflict of interest codes, and a specific advice letter (A-82-108) regarding when consultants participate in decision-making.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains incoming request (March 7, 1984 letter from Carniato) followed by FPPC response (April 5, 1984). The FPPC response does not contain traditional QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections but instead provides referrals to prior materials. OCR quality is good with minor spacing irregularities.

QUESTION: s, please do not hesitate to write with the specifics, 
we shall be happy to respond. 
REL:plh 
Enclosures

CONCLUSION: The FPPC does not provide a direct substantive conclusion in this response letter, instead referring the requester to prior correspondence, an instruction pamphlet on designating employees for conflict of interest codes, and a specific advice letter (A-82-108) regarding when consultants participate in decision-making.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC response letter to John J. Carniato regarding whether consultants must be covered under a public agency's conflict of interest code. Rather than providing a direct answer, the FPPC references prior correspondence, enclosures, and related advice letters to address the inquiry.
